ABS: Brake-state freshness must stay aligned with ESC to prevent mismatched brake or warning feedback
ACU: Crash-state ownership must not be mirrored inconsistently into body or gateway warning surfaces
ADM: Automatic-door state must stay aligned with access logic and body visibility readers
AFLS: Lighting-adaptation state must remain consistent with AHLS and body warning consumers
AHLS: High-beam permit state should not drift from AFLS and driver-visibility cues
ASM: Suspension state must stay consistent with ESC and ADAS readers during stability-related transitions
AVM: Camera-surround state must stay synchronized with parking-master and IVI consumers
BAT_BMS: Battery limits must stay aligned with VCU and charging surfaces during mode changes
BCW: Blind-spot warning state should not compete with other alert sources without clear arbitration
BIO: Biometric-auth state must remain consistent with access and security controllers
BSEC: Security and alarm state must stay aligned with access controllers and service reporting
CDC: Damping state and diagnostics can drift from chassis-health readers if update cadence diverges
CPAY: Payment-service state should stay isolated from driving-critical warning lanes
CPC: Propulsion-coordination state can become ambiguous if downstream readers infer different ownership
CSM: Cabin-sensing state must remain consistent with access and comfort decisions
DATC: Climate-state freshness must stay aligned with TMU and IVI comfort surfaces
DCDC: Converter state should stay coherent with charging and energy-status surfaces
DCM: Diagnostic state must stay aligned with gateway and service-routing visibility
DKEY: Digital-key state must stay aligned with access and service-routing surfaces
DMS: Attention state must remain consistent with HWP and warning-selection logic
DOOR_FL: Door state must remain synchronized with BCM, access logic, and comfort rendering
DOOR_FR: Door state must remain synchronized with BCM, access logic, and comfort rendering
DOOR_RL: Door state must remain synchronized with BCM, access logic, and comfort rendering
DOOR_RR: Door state must remain synchronized with BCM, access logic, and comfort rendering
ECS: Suspension health and mode state must remain synchronized across ADAS and validation readers
EDR: Event logging state must match fail-safe escalation and service readback timing
EMS: Engine and propulsion state must not diverge from the VCU-facing propulsion summary
EOP: Oil-pump support state should remain aligned with thermal and propulsion-health readers
EPB: Parking-brake state should not lag cluster output or validation-state readback
ETHB: Backbone link state must remain consistent with service-routing and ingress-egress visibility
EWP: Water-pump support state should stay synchronized with thermal-support consumers
EXT_DIAG: External diagnostic-session state must not bypass normal gateway control semantics
FCA: Collision-assist request state must stay aligned with AEB and ESC consumers
FCAM: Camera-perception freshness can affect object-fusion confidence and highway-ready decisions
FPCM: Fuel-pump control state must stay consistent with propulsion readiness and diagnostics
FRADAR: Radar-perception freshness must stay consistent with ADAS fusion and warning outputs
HLM: Headlamp-leveling state must stay consistent with lighting and body diagnostics
HUD: HUD rendering must match selected alert semantics from CLU and IVI
HWP: Highway-ready state must stay aligned with DMS, cluster, and control-activation readers
IBOX: Connectivity-hub state must stay aligned with route and service-activity readers
INVERTER: Inverter availability and fault state must stay aligned with VCU and charging readers
ISG: Starter-generator state should remain consistent with propulsion readiness and energy management surfaces
LCA: Lane-change assist state can conflict with broader warning arbitration if freshness drifts
LDR: Lidar target validity must remain aligned with fusion and path-readiness consumers
LDWS_LKAS: Lane-state freshness must stay synchronized with warning and steering-support paths
LVR: Range-selection state must not drift from drive-state ownership in the VCU lane
MCU: Motor-control state must align with the VCU propulsion summary and energy readers
MIR: Mirror state must stay aligned with body comfort visibility and door-access context
MSC: Massage-seat control state must remain consistent with seat controllers and HMI
NAV: Route-context freshness must remain aligned with ADAS and IVI consumers
OBC: Charger state must stay coherent with battery and IVI charging surfaces
ODS: Occupant state must stay consistent with BCM and CGW safety readers
OMS: Occupant-monitoring state must remain consistent with BCM and warning-selection readers
OTA: OTA service state should stay isolated from runtime warning ownership
PAK: Passive-access key state must remain synchronized with SMK and BCM access surfaces
PGS: Parking-guidance state must match parking-assist and IVI consumers
PKM: Parking-master orchestration can drift from camera and ultrasonic consumers if ownership is unclear
PTG: Tailgate-power state must stay aligned with access and body warning surfaces
PUS: Ultrasonic state must stay aligned with parking controllers and IVI visualization
RATC: Rear-climate state must remain synchronized with DATC and HMI comfort surfaces
RPC: Road-preview state should remain consistent with ADAS control-adaptation readers
RRM: Rear-radar master state must stay consistent with surround-warning consumers
RSE: Rear-seat entertainment state should stay isolated from critical alert-rendering paths
RSPA: Remote-parking state should remain synchronized with chassis and HMI consumers
RWS: Rear-steer state must stay aligned with steering and stability consumers to avoid path mismatch
SAS: Steering-angle sensor freshness is critical for ESC and MDPS consumers
SEAT_DRV: Seat state must stay consistent with comfort profiles and occupant-related readers
SEAT_PASS: Seat state must stay consistent with comfort profiles and occupant-related readers
SGW: Secure-gateway state must stay aligned with route ownership and service authorization
SMK: Smart-key and immobilizer state must remain synchronized with PAK and BCM access logic
SPAS: Parking-assist steering state must align with MDPS and parking-HMI readers
SPM: Surround-parking orchestration must stay aligned with IVI and parking-controller consumers
SRF: Sunroof state must stay aligned with body comfort and safety visibility
SRR_FL: Corner-radar freshness must stay consistent with surround fusion and parking or lane warnings
SRR_FR: Corner-radar freshness must stay consistent with surround fusion and parking or lane warnings
SRR_RL: Corner-radar freshness must stay consistent with surround fusion and parking or lane warnings
SRR_RR: Corner-radar freshness must stay consistent with surround fusion and parking or lane warnings
TCU: Transmission state must remain synchronized with longitudinal control and cluster rendering
TGM: Tailgate-position state must remain synchronized with BCM and access readers
TMU: Telematics state must remain consistent with NAV, DATC, and service-facing readers
TPMS: Tire-pressure state must remain fresh enough for warning surfaces and chassis-health logic
TRM: Trailer state must remain aligned with VCU and ADAS behavior adaptations
VCS: Voice state and audio focus must stay aligned with AMP and IVI service rendering
WIP: Wiper state and diagnostics must stay consistent with BCM and visibility surfaces
_4WD: Driveline-engagement state should stay consistent between propulsion and chassis readers
